claim: Agent observability is fundamentally different from software observability: traces (not code) are the source of truth because agent behavior is emergent.
actionable: Design your agent logging around capturing complete traces of each execution; the code alone does not reveal what agents will do.
source_title: Observability and Evals for AI Agents: A Simple Breakdown

claim: Agent evaluation differs from software testing: you test reasoning at different granularities (single step, trace, thread), not just code paths.
actionable: Structure evals at three levels: single LLM calls (unit tests), full agent runs (integration tests), and multi-turn conversations (system tests).
source_title: Observability and Evals for AI Agents: A Simple Breakdown

claim: Production is where you discover what to test offline: you don't know agent failure modes until users interact with it.
actionable: Ship agents early with good observability; use production traces to discover failure modes, then add offline tests to prevent regression.
source_title: Observability and Evals for AI Agents: A Simple Breakdown

claim: Three core primitives of agent observability: runs (single LLM calls), traces (ordered collections of runs), threads (multi-turn interactions).
actionable: Instrument your agent framework to log runs and group them into traces; organize traces across conversations into threads for context.
source_title: Observability and Evals for AI Agents: A Simple Breakdown

claim: Online evals flag issues in production without ground truth; offline evals use ground truth to catch regressions.
actionable: Use offline evals for regression testing with known correct answers; use online evals for production quality checks (trajectory, efficiency, no crashes).
source_title: Observability and Evals for AI Agents: A Simple Breakdown

claim: Observability and evals are tightly coupled in agents because traces power both debugging, offline evals, and online evals.
actionable: Build your observability system first; it will naturally feed your eval strategy and debugging workflow.
source_title: Observability and Evals for AI Agents: A Simple Breakdown

claim: Common user workflow: report bug → find production trace → extract state at failure point → create test case → fix offline → deploy.
actionable: Streamline production trace export; include tool inputs, outputs, and any relevant state snapshots so engineers can quickly replicate issues offline.
source_title: Observability and Evals for AI Agents: A Simple Breakdown
